frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: अरे बेटा, कभी-कभी ऐसा होता है। आजकल की लड़कियां वैसे भी बहुत पढ़ी-लिखी और बिज़ी रहती हैं। मैंने भी एक बार एक मैडम से बात करने की कोशिश की थी ऑफिस में, काम के सिलसिले में ही, तो वो ज़्यादा दिलचस्पी नहीं दिखा रहीं थीं। फिर बाद में पता चला कि उनकी शादी हो चुकी है, उनके पति भी IAS हैं। तो फिर मैंने भी ज़्यादा ज़ोर नहीं दिया। आदमी को समझना चाहिए कहाँ रुकना है। इज़्ज़त सबसे पहले।
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Suresh describes an instance where a woman's disinterest was not enough to stop his 'pursuit' (even if for work-related talk), but learning she was married and her husband was an IAS officer immediately made him back off. This encodes the surface manifestation of 'stopping pursuit only after learning a woman is taken, not after she said no' and 'respecting ownership'.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 20 years. A government officer in Bhopal. You are at a family dinner at your house. Your wife Meena has cooked everything herself Ñ five dishes, took her four hours. Your relatives are eating and complimenting the food. You are sitting at the head of the table and you are making a toast. You are saying something about Meena and what she means to this family. Your toast sounds loving and grateful. But every sentence in it defines Meena entirely through what she has given up and what she has cooked and what she has endured and never once mentions who she is outside of those sacrifices but only cooking glorifies her.
